target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- A rolling timer (20s cadence) shows elapsed time + a wall-clock stamp while running, so a stalled lane is visible at a glance; cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane. Tagged payloads (`<think>reason</think>answer`) keep only the in-tag reasoning, so answer prose after `</think>` never lands in the lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **Rate-limit-aware cadence** — the rolling "still running" timer ticks every 20s (not every few seconds) so N concurrently-streaming Thinking messages stay under Telegram's per-chat edit rate and don't draw 429s (a 429 drops the edit and stalls the lane); the line carries a wall-clock stamp so a genuine stall is obvious
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Prerequisite

- **#80046** (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args) — **merged 2026-05-29**; supplies the `onToolStart` lifecycle events the tool lines render. This branch should be rebased onto current `main` (which now contains it) before merge.

The renderer otherwise consumes only existing callbacks and is default-off, so it depends on no unmerged work. (On the standard `claude-cli` backend, CLI-*native* reasoning text additionally benefits from #85381's `thinking_delta`; without it the lane simply shows tool/event lines. The interactive backend restores thinking via its proxy, so reasoning appears regardless. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected — this is not a hard dependency.)

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard + wall-clock timer stamp, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append, timer-interval constant)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; 20s rolling-timer cadence; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (extract only in-`<think>` reasoning, dropping answer prose after `</think>`, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle + wall-clock stamp, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, tagged-reasoning split (answer after `</think>` dropped; streaming/code cases), defensive final-answer strip
- [x] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Verification

Local checks at the current head:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions                                                 # typecheck — clean
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts   # lint — clean
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts \
               extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.test.ts   # all green
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opt-in Telegram interleaved progress renders reasoning plus tool/progress events in one durable Thinking message, without duplicating the final answer or freezing at Telegram message limits.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw Telegram gateway against Telegram, default-off feature explicitly enabled, two concurrent Telegram threads in one chat.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt and ran the gateway from the PR head, enabled channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress, sent concurrent Telegram prompts that produced reasoning, tool progress, long Thinking output, and idle tool waits, then inspected Telegram messages and the gateway outbound Telegram send log.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot embedded below plus redacted outbound Telegram send-log metrics copied below: continuation msg=34783 -> msg=34786, final answer absent from every Thinking edit, zero bare Thinking sends, 20s timer edits at 04:20:15 -> 04:20:35 -> 04:20:55, and 99 outbound calls with 0 failures.

Observed result after fix: Thinking lane showed reasoning plus tool line, rolled into a continuation before the cap, sent the polished answer once as its own message, cleared empty Thinking output, and avoided Telegram 429s under the two-thread concurrent run.

What was not tested: No broad channel matrix or release package lane in this PR; focused local proof covers the Telegram renderer helpers, schema, lint, and extension typecheck.

## Live proof (current head)

Captured on the current head with the default-off feature enabled, over a **two-thread concurrent** session. The screenshot shows the visible lane; the rest is verified from the gateway's **outbound Telegram send log** — every `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` the bot issues — which shows message-level behavior a screenshot can't (message ids, edit cadence, final-state content, timing). Wire-level facts below are message-level metrics (ids, lengths, ok/timing) and contain no command/path/host content.

**Normal interleaving — reasoning + tool-use line in the Thinking lane, with the rolling timer:**

![interleaved lane](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/66da4d16b5a9467a82a65134976bc66aa956519c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-30-toolline.png)

**Wire-level verification (gateway send log, current head, one two-thread concurrent session):**

- **Long-message continuation** — `msg=34783` grew to the 4000-char cap and rolled into a fresh continuation `msg=34786` (carrying the next tool line) instead of freezing; clean seam, no garble.
- **No duplicate final answer** — on that long thinking-heavy turn the final answer was delivered once as its own message and is **absent from every Thinking edit** of `34783`/`34786`.
- **No empty Thinking** — **zero** bare-`Thinking`-header sends across the session.
- **20s rolling-timer cadence (rate-limit relief)** — while idling on a blocking tool, the "still running" line re-edited at **exactly 20s intervals** (04:20:15 → 04:20:35 → 04:20:55, body static at 3764 chars), keeping concurrent Thinking messages under Telegram's edit rate. The wall-clock stamp exposes any genuine stall.
- **No rate-limiting** — **99** outbound calls across the two concurrent threads, **0** failures (no `429`s, no oversized-preview stop) — the exact concurrency that previously drew 429s.

target.title: fix(browser): remove unused requireRef import and void workaround (#83878)
target.body:
## Summary

Removes the unused `requireRef` import and its `void requireRef` silencing workaround from `register.navigation.ts`. The import serves no purpose — the file does not call `requireRef`, and documenting the public surface of `shared.ts` belongs in `shared.ts` itself or a barrel export, not as a phantom import.

Closes #83878

## Change Type

- [x] Code cleanup / Dead code removal

## Scope

- [x] Browser extension / CLI

## Linked Issue

- Closes #83878

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `register.navigation.ts` imports `requireRef` from `./shared.js` but never calls it. The `void requireRef` expression at line 84 silences the unused-import lint without creating a real dependency. This is dead code that obscures the actual import graph.

**Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 25.4.0 arm64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw branch `fix/remove-void-require-ref` rebased on upstream main commit `8e110a21`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Removed `requireRef` from the import and deleted the `void requireRef` + comment block (4 lines removed, 1 line changed).
2. Verified TypeScript compilation: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json` — exit 0.
3. Verified formatting: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts` — clean.
4. Verified linting: `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors.
5. Drift proof: the removed import (`requireRef`) and `void` expression do not exist in the patched version. Reverting the patch restores the dead import.

**Evidence after fix**:

```
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json
# exit 0

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts
Finished in 22ms on 1 files using 10 threads.

$ pnpm exec oxlint extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

Diff (1 file, +1 / -4):
```diff
-import { requireRef, resolveBrowserActionContext } from "./shared.js";
+import { resolveBrowserActionContext } from "./shared.js";
...
-  // Keep `requireRef` reachable; shared utilities are intended for other modules too.
-  void requireRef;
```

**Observed result**: After the patch, `register.navigation.ts` no longer imports or references `requireRef`. TypeScript, format, and lint all pass cleanly. The file is functionally identical — `resolveBrowserActionContext` is still imported and used as before.

target.title: fix(browser): isolate Chrome MCP pending attach aborts
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88304.

- keep Chrome MCP cached-session attach cancellation scoped to the caller that cancelled
- track active waiters while a shared attach is still pending or waiting for `session.ready`
- tear down the attach only when the last waiter cancels, evict cancelled pending entries before a fresh caller can join them, and evict cached sessions before awaited close cleanup

## Motivation / root cause

`getSession()` shared one pending Chrome MCP attach per profile/cache key, but created that shared pending promise with the first caller's `AbortSignal`. If that first caller cancelled while another caller was also waiting for the same Chrome MCP session, the shared pending attach could reject with the first caller's abort reason and fail the unrelated caller.

The fix gives the shared attach its own abort controller and treats each caller as a waiter. Caller aborts release that waiter. The shared attach is only aborted or closed when no waiters remain, and last-waiter close cleanup removes the session from the cache before awaiting close so a fresh caller starts a new attach instead of reusing a handle being torn down.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: one cancelled Chrome MCP caller no longer cancels another caller waiting on the same shared pending attach; all-cancelled attaches are still cleaned up; fresh callers do not reuse a session that is already closing after last-waiter cancellation.

Real environment tested: macOS local checkout with the Chrome MCP runtime module loaded through `node --import tsx` and the real `listChromeMcpTabs` path using a test session factory.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.test.ts
node --import tsx --input-type=module  # mixed factory-pending repro/proof
node --import tsx --input-type=module  # mixed ready-pending repro/proof
node --import tsx --input-type=module  # all-aborted-before-ready cleanup proof
node --import tsx --input-type=module  # fresh caller during last-waiter close proof
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
```

Evidence before fix:

```json
{
  "first": "rejected",
  "firstReason": "first caller cancelled",
  "second": "rejected",
  "secondReason": "first caller cancelled",
  "closed": 1
}
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "proof": "mixed-factory-pending",
  "first": "rejected",
  "firstReason": "first caller cancelled",
  "second": "fulfilled",
  "secondTabs": 1,
  "closed": 0
}
```

```json
{
  "proof": "mixed-ready-pending",
  "factoryCalls": 1,
  "first": "rejected",
  "firstReason": "first caller cancelled",
  "second": "fulfilled",
  "thirdTabs": 1,
  "closed": 0
}
```

```json
{
  "proof": "all-aborted-before-ready",
  "factoryCalls": 1,
  "first": "rejected",
  "firstReason": "caller cancelled",
  "closed": 1
}
```

```json
{
  "proof": "fresh-caller-during-close",
  "factoryCalls": 2,
  "first": "rejected",
  "firstReason": "caller cancelled",
  "secondTabs": 1,
  "closeCounts": [
    1,
    0
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: a remaining waiter can complete and later calls reuse the same session; when every waiter cancels before readiness, the session handle is closed exactly once; when a fresh caller arrives while the old session close is still awaiting, it starts a new attach and does not reuse the closing session.

What was not tested: a live Chrome browser/CDP process was not launched for this proof. The exercised path is the Chrome MCP runtime session cache and cancellation behavior with a controlled session factory.

## Regression tests

Added focused coverage in `extensions/browser/src/browser/chrome-mcp.test.ts` for:

- one waiter aborting while another waiter remains during factory attach;
- every waiter aborting before the factory resolves;
- a fresh caller after an all-waiters-aborted pending attach;
- every waiter aborting while `session.ready` is pending;
- a fresh caller arriving while last-waiter abort cleanup is still awaiting `close()`;
- a mixed ready-pending wait where one caller aborts, another succeeds, and a later call reuses the same session.

## Compatibility / risks

This only changes Chrome MCP cached-session cancellation and cleanup behavior. The main risk is around session lifecycle races, so the patch keeps stale-session, profile-key rotation, and transport-error cleanup behavior intact and adds tests for the cancellation cases that can otherwise leak, duplicate, or reuse closing sessions.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the CLI run and request-log assertion:

```text
[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] startup stages: runId=c43c486b-23d0-4e83-8fd1-c0dd12f67cba sessionId=proof-subagent-session phase=attempt-dispatch totalMs=5818 stages=workspace:1ms@1ms,runtime-plugins:5605ms@5606ms,hooks:6ms@5612ms,model-resolution:113ms@5725ms,auth:67ms@5792ms,context-engine:1ms@5793ms,attempt-workspace:1ms@5794ms,attempt-prompt:0ms@5794ms,attempt-runtime-plan:24ms@5818ms,attempt-dispatch:0ms@5818ms
```

```json
{
  "head": "25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": ["exec", "read"],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds echo delivery infrastructure that mirrors session messages to additional channels
- `SessionEchoTarget` type on `SessionEntry` for persistent echo target configuration
- `message:sent` internal hook triggers echo delivery (fire-and-forget, no circular echo)
- CLI: `openclaw sessions echo add/remove/list` subcommands
- Gateway method: `sessions.echo` for programmatic pin/unpin (VituReClaw HUD)

## Motivation

Sessions used across multiple transports (Telegram + VituReClaw glasses + web UI) result in fragmented chat logs. Echo targets let every turn appear in all pinned channels regardless of originating transport.

## Files changed

| File | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/sessions/types.ts` | `SessionEchoTarget` type + `echoTargets` field |
| `src/infra/outbound/echo.ts` | Core echo resolution + delivery |
| `src/infra/outbound/echo-hook.ts` | Internal hook registration |
| `src/commands/sessions-echo.ts` | CLI subcommands |
| `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts` | `sessions.echo` gateway method |
| `src/gateway/server-methods.ts` | Method registration |
| `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts` | Hook auto-registration at boot |
| `src/cli/program/register.status-health-sessions.ts` | CLI command wiring |

## Test plan

- [ ] Unit test for `resolveEchoTargets` (deduplication, role filtering)
- [ ] Integration test: add echo target via gateway method, send message, verify echo arrives
- [ ] Verify circular echo prevention (echo delivery does not re-trigger hooks)
- [ ] CLI smoke test: add/list/remove echo targets

Supersedes #88386 (closed: branch had unrelated deployment commits).

target.title: fix(browser): remove unused requireRef import and void workaround (#83878)
target.body:
## Summary

Removes the unused `requireRef` import and its `void requireRef` silencing workaround from `register.navigation.ts`. The import serves no purpose — the file does not call `requireRef`, and documenting the public surface of `shared.ts` belongs in `shared.ts` itself or a barrel export, not as a phantom import.

Closes #83878

## Change Type

- [x] Code cleanup / Dead code removal

## Scope

- [x] Browser extension / CLI

## Linked Issue

- Closes #83878

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `register.navigation.ts` imports `requireRef` from `./shared.js` but never calls it. The `void requireRef` expression at line 84 silences the unused-import lint without creating a real dependency. This is dead code that obscures the actual import graph.

**Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 25.4.0 arm64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw branch `fix/remove-void-require-ref` rebased on upstream main commit `8e110a21`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Removed `requireRef` from the import and deleted the `void requireRef` + comment block (4 lines removed, 1 line changed).
2. Verified TypeScript compilation: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json` — exit 0.
3. Verified formatting: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts` — clean.
4. Verified linting: `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors.
5. Drift proof: the removed import (`requireRef`) and `void` expression do not exist in the patched version. Reverting the patch restores the dead import.

**Evidence after fix**:

```
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json
# exit 0

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts
Finished in 22ms on 1 files using 10 threads.

$ pnpm exec oxlint extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

Diff (1 file, +1 / -4):
```diff
-import { requireRef, resolveBrowserActionContext } from "./shared.js";
+import { resolveBrowserActionContext } from "./shared.js";
...
-  // Keep `requireRef` reachable; shared utilities are intended for other modules too.
-  void requireRef;
```

**Observed result**: After the patch, `register.navigation.ts` no longer imports or references `requireRef`. TypeScript, format, and lint all pass cleanly. The file is functionally identical — `resolveBrowserActionContext` is still imported and used as before.

target.title: fix(agents): count streamed model deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary
- Count streamed `text_delta`/`thinking_delta`/`toolcall_delta` diagnostics from their incremental `delta` bytes instead of serializing the full event.
- Drop growing `partial` snapshots from fallback stream byte accounting so diagnostics do not repeatedly walk the accumulated assistant message.
- Add regression coverage proving text-delta accounting does not serialize large partial snapshots and keeps streams alive if byte inspection throws.
- Document the bounded `responseStreamBytes` semantics for high-frequency streamed deltas and align OTEL docs/metric descriptions with that bounded payload contract.

Refs #86599.

## Verification
- Rebasing onto current `origin/main` completed on head `7af7838e562`; obsolete unrelated gate-unblocker commits were dropped because their patch contents are already upstream.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed after rebase.

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts --reporter=dot` - passed after rebase, 2 shards / 86 tests.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean after rebase, no accepted/actionable findings, `overall: patch is correct (0.86)`.
- Previous AWS Crabbox `check:changed`, provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4605e66e6f2f`, run `run_e94bc87a3cea`, base `b7e9272dbec`, head `013fb59dc0e`: exit 0. Post-rebase GitHub CI is the current remote gate for head `7af7838e562`.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: dense model streams no longer make model-call diagnostics serialize the full growing `partial` assistant message on every delta.
Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree after rebase; earlier AWS Crabbox changed gate before the final rebase; GitHub CI is running on the rebased head.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/gateway/opentelemetry.md docs/logging.md`; `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/diagnostics-otel/src/service.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --threads=1`.

What was not tested: a live long-running local-model session; the fix is validated through the diagnostics stream accounting path and changed-gate/CI proof.

target.title: fix(telegram): silently skip empty-text and HTML-whitespace sends
target.body:
Supersedes #59725. The original PR was auto-closed on 2026-04-02 by openclaw-barnacle for the 10-open-PR cap. Reopening it programmatically fails because the close `stateReason` is `COMPLETED` plus the branch was force-pushed since, so this is a fresh PR carrying the rebased fix. ## Behavior addressed Interrupted mid-reply turns can stream bare `
`, `
`, `
`, or ` ` chunks. The Telegram Bot API rejects these with HTTP 400 (`message text is empty` legacy, `text must be non-empty` newer wording). Previously this surfaced as a user-visible delivery failure; this PR silently skips the send instead. ## Change Two commits: - `24ba9891c6` (`extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.send.ts` + colocated test) — the silent-skip itself. - `07dd75c81e` (`extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot/reply-threading.ts`, new `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.skip-accounting.test.ts`) — skipped-send accounting fix in the caller, addressing the ClawSweeper P1 finding on this PR. Behavior in `delivery.send.ts`: - `sendTelegramText` return type widened to `Promise`. Both call sites in `delivery.replies.ts` already handle `undefined` via `if (firstDeliveredMessageId == null)`. - Pre-flight branch: when formatted HTML trims to empty AND `plainText` trims to empty, log via `logVerbose` and return `undefined`. - Post-call branch: when Telegram replies with the empty-text 400 AND there is no plain fallback, log via `logVerbose` and return `undefined`. - `EMPTY_TEXT_ERR_RE` now matches both `message text is empty` and `text must be non-empty`. - All other Telegram client rejections still throw unchanged. Non-empty payloads still deliver via the existing markdown / plain-fallback pipeline. Behavior in `delivery.replies.ts` + `reply-threading.ts`: - `sendChunkedTelegramReplyText`'s `sendChunk` callback now returns `Promise`; when it resolves `undefined`, the loop skips `markReplyApplied` and `markDelivered` for that chunk. This keeps `progress.hasReplied`, `progress.hasDelivered`, and `progress.deliveredCount` untouched for skipped sends so the downstream `transcriptMirror`, `message_sent` hook (`success=true` + real `messageId`), and `maybePinFirstDeliveredMessage` only run for chunks that actually delivered. - `sendTelegramVoiceFallbackText`'s per-chunk loop gets the same guard: skipped sends do not flip `appliedReplyTo` or stash a phantom `firstDeliveredMessageId`, so a skipped first chunk does not strand the reply reference, quote text, or buttons. ## Real behavior proof **Behavior addressed:** Telegram delivery layer raises a hard 400 failure when interrupted mid-reply turns stream bare `
`, `
`, `
`, or ` ` chunks that Telegram strips to empty server-side and returns either the "message text is empty" or "text must be non-empty" 400 description. **Real environment tested:** Live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The fix is deployed and active. Verification driver imports the deployed `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.send.ts` directly from the gateway's working tree via the `tsx` runtime so the exact code the gateway executes is exercised. **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ``` ssh alexm@rh-bot.lan 'cd /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw && node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/proof-empty-text.mts' ``` **Evidence after fix:** ``` host: rh-bot.lan gateway-pid: 65734 build-SHA: 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28) node-version: 25.8.2 --- PASS pre-flight skip both empty | calls=0 result=undefined PASS bare
chunk + empty plain | calls=1 result=undefined PASS self-closing
| calls=1 result=undefined PASS spaced
| calls=1 result=undefined PASS nbsp-only | calls=1 result=undefined PASS multiple nbsp | calls=1 result=undefined PASS new wording | calls=1 result=undefined PASS unrelated 400 still throws | calls=1 result=throw:400: Bad Request: chat not found PASS well-formed payload still delivers | calls=1 result=9999 --- result: 9 pass / 0 fail ``` **Observed result after fix:** The pre-flight whitespace-only payload short-circuits with zero API calls (calls=0). Each of the six HTML-stripped-to-empty payloads (bare `
`, `
`, `
`, ` `, `   `, ``) makes exactly one API call (calls=1), the documented 400 wording is caught, and the silent skip fires returning `undefined`. The newer `text must be non-empty` wording is caught alongside the legacy `message text is empty` wording. Unrelated 400 errors such as "chat not found" still throw to the caller (calls=1 result=throw). Well-formed payloads still deliver with a real message_id (calls=1 result=9999). **What was not tested:** Live Telegram round-trip with a real bot token sending the malformed payloads to a real chat (covered by the second proof transcript below). --- ### Live Telegram round-trip proof (added in response to ClawSweeper review) **Real environment tested (live Telegram round-trip):** Live Telegram Bot API call against `@requestymcrequestfacebot` (bot id 8101720064) sending to Alex's DM. Driver script `tsx`-imports the actual source-tree `sendTelegramText` from this PR branch (`extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.send.ts` at SHA `5480ad003`) and drives it through the real grammY `Bot` constructed against the live token. No mocks, no recorded fixtures — every request hits `api.telegram.org`. **Steps run against Telegram Bot API:** ``` PROOF_BOT_TOKEN= \ PROOF_CHAT_ID= \ node_modules/.bin/tsx /tmp/openclaw-pr88810-proof/proof-empty-text-skip.mts ``` The script does three things end to end: 1. Raw grammY `bot.api.sendMessage(chatId, payload, { parse_mode: "HTML" })` for each candidate, recording the live Telegram response. This sets the baseline for what Telegram actually does with each payload today. 2. Source-tree `sendTelegramText(bot, chatId, payload, runtime, { textMode: "html", plainText: "" })` against the same payloads. `plainText:""` matches the silent-skip pre-flight gate so the skip branch is reachable. 3. One well-formed payload through `sendTelegramText` to confirm normal delivery still returns a real message_id. **Evidence after fix (live API capture, bot token and chat id redacted):** ``` === step 1: raw grammY bot.api.sendMessage with parse_mode=HTML === payload="
" expected=parse-error -> error_code=400 description="Bad Request: can't parse entities: Unsupported start tag \"br\" at byte offset 0" payload="
" expected=parse-error -> error_code=400 description="Bad Request: can't parse entities: Unsupported start tag \"br/\" at byte offset 0" payload="
" expected=parse-error -> error_code=400 description="Bad Request: can't parse entities: Unsupported start tag \"br\" at byte offset 0" payload=" " expected=accepted-by-telegram -> OK message_id=10657 payload="   " expected=accepted-by-telegram -> OK message_id=10658 payload="" expected=empty-text -> error_code=400 description="Bad Request: text must be non-empty" === step 2: source-tree sendTelegramText wrapper with plainText='' === payload="
" expected=parse-error -> THREW error_code=400 description="Bad Request: can't parse entities: Unsupported start tag \"br\" at byte offset 0" elapsedMs=134 payload="
" expected=parse-error -> THREW error_code=400 description="Bad Request: can't parse entities: Unsupported start tag \"br/\" at byte offset 0" elapsedMs=123 payload="
" expected=parse-error -> THREW error_code=400 description="Bad Request: can't parse entities: Unsupported start tag \"br\" at byte offset 0" elapsedMs=115 runtime.log: telegram sendMessage ok chat= message=10659 payload=" " expected=accepted-by-telegram -> resolved=10659 elapsedMs=273 runtime.log: telegram sendMessage ok chat= message=10660 payload="   " expected=accepted-by-telegram -> resolved=10660 elapsedMs=260 payload="" expected=empty-text -> resolved=undefined elapsedMs=128 === step 3: real non-empty payload still delivers a Telegram message_id === runtime.log: telegram sendMessage ok chat= message=10661 deliverable payload resolved=10661 (real Telegram message_id) === done === ``` **Observed result after fix (live API capture):** - Step 1 baseline reveals the live Telegram Bot API splits the candidates into three buckets: - `
`, `
`, `
` return a parse-error 400 (`can't parse entities`), not the empty-text 400. - ` ` and `   ` are accepted by Telegram and rendered as visible text (message ids 10657, 10658). - `` returns the empty-text 400 (`text must be non-empty`) the PR's silent-skip path is designed to catch. - Step 2 confirms the source-tree `sendTelegramText` from this PR: - resolves `undefined` for `` (silent skip, exactly the PR's target behavior, with the new wording `text must be non-empty` matched by the widened `EMPTY_TEXT_ERR_RE`), - resolves the real Telegram message id for ` ` payloads (10659, 10660) since Telegram accepts them, - and still throws the parse-error 400 for bare `
` family payloads when no plain fallback is provided, which is intentional and outside this PR's scope (parse errors are not empty-text errors). - Step 3 sends a well-formed payload (message id 10661), confirming the silent-skip branch did not regress the happy path. **What was not tested:** The deliverReplies caller path is exercised by the new `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.skip-accounting.test.ts` regression suite (3 tests, all passing), not by the live Telegram driver above. Driving an empty post-strip chunk all the way through `chunkText -> filterEmptyTelegramTextChunks -> sendChunkedTelegramReplyText -> sendTelegramText` against a live bot is not feasible because the `filterEmptyTelegramTextChunks` step would drop any chunk whose `text.trim()` is empty before it ever reaches the silent-skip branch in production; the regression test mocks `sendTelegramText` itself so the integrated accounting can be observed. ## Tests ``` node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot ``` Result: 6 test files, 183 tests, all passing (includes the new `delivery.skip-accounting.test.ts` with 3 tests covering pin path, hooks, mirror, and delivered flag on skipped sends).

target.title: fix(agents): stream phased text deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- stop rereading full OpenAI Responses phased partial text on every same-item `final_answer` delta
- keep the same user-visible sanitizer path for incremental phased deltas, including split tool-call XML payloads
- preserve full-partial reads for item boundaries and `text_end`, where the stream needs authoritative final text

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.emits-block-replies-text-end-does-not.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.filters-final-suppresses-output-without-start-tag.test.ts --reporter=dot`: passed 1 shard / 69 tests after rebasing onto `origin/main` `fda5254e99f`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`: passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts`: passed
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`: passed
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --label delta-stream-rebased-check-changed --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (`provider=aws`, `lease=cbx_65af4f6bbebc`, `slug=swift-prawn`, `run=run_e604562888c1`, exit 0)
- GitHub PR checks on head `8cc28f2de02db915a2e20d69a0f3e7a9927e2960`: latest poll had no failed checks; CI was still running

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: same-item phased OpenAI Responses final-answer deltas stream incrementally without rereading full partial assistant text, while sanitizer context is preserved for hidden split tool-call payloads.

Real environment tested: linked OpenClaw gwt worktree, focused local Vitest wrapper, AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate, and GitHub PR CI on the pushed head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: reran focused subscriber regression tests, formatter, lint, diff check, remote `pnpm check:changed`, and PR checks.

Evidence after fix: local subscriber tests passed 69 tests; Crabbox run `run_e604562888c1` selected `core, coreTests`, including core/core-test typecheck, changed-file lint, import-cycle checks, and guards, then exited 0.

Observed result after fix: repeated same-item final-answer deltas emit appended user-visible text without touching the full partial text getter; split hidden tool-call XML does not leak arguments into assistant events.

What was not tested: true live Ollama UI repro; this PR covers the deterministic subscriber hot path behind the reported #86599 stream-density issue.

target.title: test(ui): remove stylesheet grep tests
target.body:
## Summary

This removes the raw stylesheet substring tests under `ui/src/styles/**` and keeps one replacement browser/computed-style guard for the highest-value behavior those tests covered: mobile text controls staying large enough to avoid iOS focus zoom.

The deleted tests were fragile because they asserted CSS source implementation details rather than rendered UI behavior. Most of them read stylesheet files and checked exact selector names, declaration substrings, or broad regex matches over CSS blocks. That makes harmless CSS refactors fail CI even when the UI still renders correctly, while real layout regressions can still pass as long as the expected string remains somewhere in the stylesheet.

The tradeoff here is intentionally reducing broad, shallow coverage in favor of one small rendered-style check. I kept the iOS/mobile form-control guard because it came from a concrete browser behavior and is cheap to validate with computed font sizes. I did not replace the other selector-presence/layout-string tests one-for-one because doing so recreated too much test surface for low signal, and existing browser/layout tests already cover the more important chat and sessions responsive flows.

Net effect: fewer brittle stylesheet contract tests, no production CSS changes, and a focused browser-style regression for the most user-visible behavior that was worth preserving.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/form-controls.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/chat-responsive.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.browser.test.ts` - passed, browser tests skipped locally because this checkout has no Chromium executable
- `node ../node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config vitest.config.ts src/ui/form-controls.browser.test.ts src/ui/chat/chat-responsive.browser.test.ts src/ui/views/sessions.browser.test.ts` - passed, skipped locally for the same Chromium guard
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/styles --passWithNoTests` - passed, expected empty target after deleting the style grep tests
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/vitest.config.ts ui/src/ui/form-controls.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/chat-responsive.browser.test.ts` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings

target.title: fix(telegram): slow polling restart storms
target.body:
## Summary
- Increase Telegram polling restart backoff from the tight 2s/30s policy to a bounded 30s/10m exponential policy.
- Deduplicate runner/bot stop calls per polling restart cycle so watchdog restarts do not stack repeated stop requests.
- Add stop-timeout burst cooldown and reset restart state after healthy normal and isolated-ingress polling cycles, including isolated forced-restart stop timeouts.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** Consecutive Telegram polling stop timeouts no longer restart on the same short 30s ceiling. The second stop-timeout burst applies a separate cooldown, while a healthy normal or isolated polling cycle resets restart attempts back to the first-delay path.
- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout `/Users/andy/openclaw` on branch `codex/telegram-poll-restart-backoff`, running the actual Telegram polling restart-delay helper and the actual `TelegramPollingSession` normal/isolated restart loops with fake Telegram worker/bot objects so no production token is required.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the helper proof below through `node --import tsx`, then ran focused Vitest cases against `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` that exercise normal restart backoff, isolated `poll-success` reset, and two consecutive isolated forced stop-timeout restarts reaching the cooldown policy.
- **Evidence after fix:** Helper output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
firstStopTimeoutDelayMs=33000
firstStopTimeoutSuffix=""
secondStopTimeoutDelayMs=132000
secondStopTimeoutSuffix=" Stop timeout burst=2; applying cooldown."
afterHealthyDelayMs=33000
afterHealthySuffix=""
```

- **Production-like isolated polling proof:**

```text
$ node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "drains Telegram delivery queue after isolated ingress reports poll success|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|uses backoff helpers|resets restart backoff|cools down repeated|forces a restart when polling stalls without getUpdates activity|forces a restart when the runner task is pending" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000

 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/andy/openclaw

··----··-·-------------------------···---------------

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed | 45 skipped (53)
   Start at  17:59:20
   Duration  3.31s (transform 1.51s, setup 73ms, import 101ms, tests 3.07s, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first stop timeout uses the normal 30s-class restart policy, the second consecutive stop timeout is raised by the stop-timeout cooldown path, the normal healthy cycle resets to the first-attempt delay, isolated-ingress `poll-success` resets the real session backoff state, and isolated forced stop-timeout restarts now wait through the same cooldown/backoff path instead of immediately continuing.
- **What was not tested:** No live Telegram network-flap run was performed because this checkout has no Telegram bot token/session available. The full local `polling-session.test.ts` file also hits an existing `upstream/main` hang in `keeps active spooled lanes blocked across isolated ingress restarts`; this was reproduced in a detached `upstream/main` worktree, so verification used focused polling-session cases plus `monitor.test`.

## Verification
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "drains Telegram delivery queue after isolated ingress reports poll success|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|uses backoff helpers|resets restart backoff|cools down repeated|forces a restart when polling stalls without getUpdates activity|forces a restart when the runner task is pending" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|restarts isolated ingress when worker liveness stalls|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|cools down repeated" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/monitor.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`

Refs #88256

target.title: fix(env): propagate service-managed GH_TOKEN to trusted exec
target.body: ## Summary - Problem: `~/.openclaw/.env` could not be used as a durable source for operator-curated tool secrets like `GH_TOKEN` because override-only-dangerous keys were stripped before the managed gateway service could expose them to trusted exec children. - Why it matters: operators can configure `gh` and similar CLI tools for OpenClaw, but after gateway install/restart those tools could not see `GH_TOKEN` through the managed service path, so authenticated CLI workflows failed unless the token was injected some other way. - What changed: allow `~/.openclaw/.env` to retain override-only-blocked secrets as managed service keys, add a trusted exec inherited-env allowlist sourced from `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MANAGED_ENV_KEYS`, and document the durable service env behavior. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): everywhere-dangerous env vars such as `LD_PRELOAD`, `NODE_OPTIONS`, `BASH_ENV`, `DYLD_*`, and `GIT_DIR` stay blocked. `OPENCLAW_*` keys still do not gain trusted exec inheritance. Untrusted exec postures do not receive these secrets. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix - [x] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [x] Skills / tool execution - [x] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: `GH_TOKEN` placed in `~/.openclaw/.env` should survive managed gateway install/restart and be available to trusted gateway exec children, so `gh` can authenticate without manually injecting the token into each command. - Real environment tested: macOS OpenClaw gateway installed as a managed service, local OpenClaw checkout from v2026.5.2 with this patch applied, `GH_TOKEN` present in `~/.openclaw/.env`, Telegram/agent exec path using `host=gateway`, `security=full`, `ask=off`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```sh cp ~/.env ~/.openclaw/.env chmod 600 ~/.openclaw/.env openclaw gateway stop openclaw gateway install --force openclaw gateway start openclaw gateway status ``` then ask the agent to run `gh auth status` and report back - Evidence after fix (screenshot): - Telegram screenshot showing the live exec environment reporting `GH_TOKEN_present: true`, `service_managed_keys_contains_GH_TOKEN: true`, `gh auth status` logged in via `GH_TOKEN`, and `gh api user` returning the authenticated account JSON. Account details and token values are redacted. - Observed result after fix: trusted gateway exec can use `GH_TOKEN` inherited from the managed service env, and `gh` authenticates successfully from the OpenClaw exec environment. - What was not tested: Linux/systemd real-service behavior was not manually tested; the Linux path is covered by the shared service-env/unit tests. Non-trusted exec postures were verified by targeted unit tests, not by a second live service screenshot. The exec integration test (`bash-tools.exec-trusted-env.exec.test.ts`) is skipped on Windows (shell-dependent); unit-level coverage runs on all platforms. - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): - `before-1.jpg` and `before-2.jpg` — terminal screenshots showing the pre-fix managed-service behavior where the gateway env path stripped `GH_TOKEN` before it could reach the exec child, so `gh` could not authenticate after service install/restart. ## Root Cause (if applicable) - Root cause: `~/.openclaw/.env` parsing treated keys that are dangerous as agent request overrides the same as keys that are dangerous everywhere. This stripped operator-curated tool tokens like `GH_TOKEN` before the managed service could record them as durable env. Separately, exec env sanitization had no trusted-mode inherited-key allowlist, so there was no narrow path for managed service keys to reach trusted tool children. - Missing detection / guardrail: existing tests covered dangerous env stripping and service env formatting, but did not cover the desired operator-curated secret path from `~/.openclaw/.env` through managed service metadata into trusted gateway exec. - Contributing context (if known): the security model already distinguished inherited/env override risk, but the durable service env path did not preserve that distinction. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `src/config/state-dir-dotenv.test.ts` - `src/daemon/service-managed-env.test.ts` - `src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts` - `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-trusted-env.test.ts` - `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-trusted-env.exec.test.ts` - `src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: `GH_TOKEN` and similar override-only-blocked keys can be retained from owner-controlled durable service env, become managed service keys, and reach exec children only in the trusted gateway posture (`host=gateway`, `security=full`, `ask=off`). Unsafe keys and `OPENCLAW_*` keys remain blocked. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: unit tests cover each policy seam, and the exec integration test proves the env-composition wiring reaches the child process under the relevant exec postures without requiring live GitHub credentials in CI. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A. - If no new test is added, why not: N/A, new tests were added. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Operators can place `GH_TOKEN` in `~/.openclaw/.env`, reinstall/start the managed gateway, and use `gh` from trusted gateway exec. This does not grant those tokens to untrusted exec postures. ## Diagram (if applicable) ```text Before: ~/.openclaw/.env GH_TOKEN -> durable env parser strips override-only-dangerous key -> managed gateway service has no GH_TOKEN-managed allowlist entry -> trusted exec child cannot authenticate gh After: ~/.openclaw/.env GH_TOKEN -> durable env parser keeps operator-curated override-only key -> install records GH_TOKEN in OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MANAGED_ENV_KEYS -> trusted gateway exec allowlist permits inherited GH_TOKEN -> gh authenticates from the exec child ``` ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No. - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes. - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No. - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes. - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes. - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this intentionally allows operator-curated service env secrets like `GH_TOKEN` to reach trusted gateway exec children. The path is constrained to keys recorded by the managed service env metadata and to the trusted exec posture. Everywhere-dangerous keys are still stripped, `OPENCLAW_*` keys are excluded, request overrides remain sanitized, and non-trusted postures do not inherit the allowlisted secret. ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS - Runtime/container: local managed OpenClaw gateway service - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): OpenClaw agent exec path, GitHub CLI (`gh`) as the real CLI consumer - Relevant config (redacted): `GH_TOKEN` present in `~/.openclaw/.env`; `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MANAGED_ENV_KEYS` included `GH_TOKEN`; token value redacted in all evidence. ### Steps 1. Put `GH_TOKEN` in `~/.openclaw/.env` and set owner-only permissions. 2. Reinstall/restart the managed gateway service with this patch applied. 3. Run a trusted gateway exec env probe and `gh auth status` / `gh api user`. 4. Run the targeted regression tests: ```sh pnpm test src/daemon/service-managed-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-trusted-env.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-trusted-env.exec.test.ts src/config/state-dir-dotenv.test.ts src/infra/host-env-security.test.ts src/commands/daemon-install-helpers.test.ts ``` ### Expected - `process.env.GH_TOKEN` is present inside trusted gateway exec. - `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MANAGED_ENV_KEYS` contains `GH_TOKEN`. - `gh auth status` reports authentication via `GH_TOKEN`. - Untrusted exec postures do not receive the fixture token in tests. - Everywhere-dangerous and `OPENCLAW_*` keys remain blocked. ### Actual - Live evidence showed `GH_TOKEN_present: true` and `service_managed_keys_contains_GH_TOKEN: true`. - `gh auth status` authenticated via `GH_TOKEN` and `gh api user` succeeded with redacted account output. - Targeted tests covered the parser, managed service allowlist, host env sanitizer, daemon install plan, and exec wiring. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) Evidence files prepared for upload: - `before-1.jpg` - `before-2.jpg` - `after-1.jpg` ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Managed gateway install/restart picks up `GH_TOKEN` from `~/.openclaw/.env`. - Trusted gateway exec sees `GH_TOKEN` and `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MANAGED_ENV_KEYS` includes `GH_TOKEN`. - `gh auth status` and `gh api user` work from the OpenClaw exec environment using `GH_TOKEN`. - Targeted tests exercise trusted, allowlist, and prompted exec postures plus dangerous-key exclusions. - Edge cases checked: - `OPENCLAW_*` keys are excluded from trusted inherited allowlist. - Everywhere-dangerous keys remain blocked even if listed. - PATH override smuggling remains blocked. - Default sanitizer behavior remains strict without `allowInheritedKeys`. - What you did **not** verify: - Manual Linux/systemd live service verification. - Every provider token named in the override-only dangerous list; `GH_TOKEN` is the live proof case and tests use representative fixture keys. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes. - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes. - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) Yes, for operators who want this behavior. - If yes, exact upgrade steps: place `GH_TOKEN` or similar operator-curated tool secret in `~/.openclaw/.env`, ensure the file is owner-only, then reinstall/restart the managed gateway service so `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MANAGED_ENV_KEYS` is regenerated. ## Risks and Mitigations - Risk: secrets that were previously stripped from managed service env can now reach trusted exec children. - Mitigation: only operator-curated durable service env keys are considered, only trusted gateway exec posture receives the inherited allowlist, and dangerous-everywhere plus `OPENCLAW_*` keys remain blocked. - Risk: operators may assume all exec postures receive `GH_TOKEN` after placing it in `~/.openclaw/.env`. - Mitigation: docs state that the exec layer decides inheritance by posture and that these keys are not auto-propagated to untrusted commands.

target.title: fix(reply): preserve sessions_send external routes
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88044.

- preserve a target session's persisted external delivery context for internal `sessions_send` handoffs that enter through the internal `webchat` surface
- carry persisted `deliveryContext.threadId` for inherited external routes so threaded transports stay on the stored thread
- mark inherited external routes in `resolveEffectiveReplyRoute` so dispatch can route them without making `sessions_send` look like a Feishu/Telegram/etc. user turn
- keep ordinary webchat turns on their live webchat route, and keep the stale-thread guard for non-inherited routes

## Behavior

Before this change, an agent-to-agent `sessions_send` turn could arrive as internal `webchat` and then fail to deliver a reply back to the existing Feishu session, even when the target session still had a valid external `deliveryContext`.

After this change, only `sessions_send` inter-session handoffs with a persisted deliverable external `deliveryContext` inherit that external route. If the persisted route has a `threadId`, dispatch passes it to `routeReply` only for that inherited route case. Normal Control UI/webchat turns continue to use their live webchat route, and ordinary routed replies still derive thread context from the active turn or thread-scoped session key.

## Verification

- `node --require ./node_modules/tsx/dist/preflight.cjs --import ./node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs .artifacts/proof/sessions-send-feishu-route-proof.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Focused threaded regression on `b024b9902b`: the route resolver now preserves `deliveryContext.threadId="thread:om_123"` for an inherited `sessions_send` external route, dispatch passes that same thread id to `routeReply`, and the existing stale-thread regression still proves ordinary/non-inherited routes do not resurrect a normalized stored thread id.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_send` replies can route through a target session's persisted external delivery context instead of being trapped as internal `webchat`; inherited external routes also preserve the persisted route thread when present.

Real environment tested: original issue #88044 reports a real OpenClaw 2026.5.27 Feishu/Lark setup with 7 agents, `tools.agentToAgent.enabled=true`, and `tools.sessions.visibility="all"`. The after-fix proof ran on patched branch `b024b9902b` using that same route shape: target session key `agent:B:feishu:B:direct:ou_xxx`, persisted `deliveryContext.channel="feishu"`, incoming `InputProvenance.kind="inter_session"`, `sourceTool="sessions_send"`, and live provider/surface/originating channel all `webchat`. To avoid live credentials in the PR, the proof registered a Feishu outbound adapter and exercised the production effective-route resolver plus durable external-channel delivery boundary.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node --require ./node_modules/tsx/dist/preflight.cjs --import ./node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs .artifacts/proof/sessions-send-feishu-route-proof.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "inputProvenance": {
    "kind": "inter_session",
    "sourceTool": "sessions_send",
    "sourceChannel": "webchat"
  },
  "sessionKey": "agent:B:feishu:B:direct:ou_xxx",
  "persistedDeliveryContext": {
    "channel": "feishu",
    "to": "user:ou_123",
    "accountId": "work"
  },
  "liveTurnSurface": {
    "Provider": "webchat",
    "Surface": "webchat",
    "OriginatingChannel": "webchat",
    "OriginatingTo": "session:dashboard"
  },
  "effectiveRoute": {
    "channel": "feishu",
    "to": "user:ou_123",
    "accountId": "work",
    "inheritedExternalRoute": true
  },
  "durableSendResult": {
    "status": "sent",
    "results": [
      {
        "channel": "feishu",
        "messageId": "om_after_fix_proof_1",
        "chatId": "user:ou_123"
      }
    ]
  },
  "feishuSends": [
    {
      "adapter": "registered feishu outbound.sendText",
      "kind": "text",
      "to": "user:ou_123",
      "accountId": "work",
      "text": "QA-SESSIONS-SEND-FEISHU-PROOF",
      "replyToId": null,
      "threadId": null
    }
  ],
  "observed": "PASS: sessions_send webchat handoff used persisted Feishu deliveryContext"
}
```

Threaded regression evidence after fix: `src/auto-reply/reply/effective-reply-route.test.ts` covers inherited `sessions_send` external route resolution with `deliveryContext.threadId="thread:om_123"`, and `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` verifies `routeReply` receives that inherited thread id. The same dispatch test file still passes the stale-thread guard case where ordinary routed replies must not read `deliveryContext.threadId` from normalized session store state.

Observed result after fix: the same route shape from #88044 now resolves the final reply route to `channel="feishu"`, `to="user:ou_123"`, `accountId="work"`, marks it `inheritedExternalRoute=true`, and the external-channel delivery boundary calls the registered Feishu outbound adapter with the proof marker. The focused regression suite also passed 198 tests and confirms normal webchat turns still stay on `webchat` while inherited threaded routes preserve `threadId`.

What was not tested: a fresh live Feishu tenant run against this patched branch was not rerun; the live Feishu evidence remains the original issue's redacted runtime logs, paired here with after-fix patched-branch external-channel delivery proof for the same persisted Feishu route shape and focused threaded route/dispatch regression coverage.

target.title: fix(doctor): respect explicit PI runtime policy
target.body:
## Summary

Respect the resolved OpenAI runtime policy before warning that the Codex plugin is missing.

This keeps the OpenAI/Codex default behavior unchanged, but makes explicit PI/OpenClaw and OpenAI-compatible routing authoritative in the diagnostics path:

- config validation suppresses only intentional missing `plugins.entries.codex` noise when the resolved OpenAI route does not use Codex
- doctor stale-plugin diagnostics keep reporting real stale policy refs such as `plugins.allow` / `plugins.deny` and explicitly enabled missing Codex entries
- shared OpenAI routing now normalizes provider ids before checking custom `baseUrl`, so `OpenAI` and `openai` behave consistently
- model policy precedence now matches the runtime contract: exact agent/default policy beats provider model defaults, and custom OpenAI-compatible bases stay out of the Codex/plugin path

Refs #88706.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: intentional PI/OpenClaw runtime policy and custom OpenAI-compatible routes no longer produce missing `plugins.entries.codex` diagnostics, while true Codex references still warn.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout at commit `c5a84de4caa`, isolated temp `OPENCLAW_HOME` configs, bundled plugins disabled with `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1` to prove the missing-plugin diagnostic path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `pnpm openclaw --no-color doctor` against four isolated configs: explicit PI route with `plugins.entries.codex`, provider model `auto/default`, custom OpenAI-compatible route with both `plugins.entries.codex` and `plugins.allow`, and explicitly enabled missing Codex entry.

Evidence after fix:

```text
routewide:
  no codex warning lines

provider_model_auto_warns:
  plugins.entries.codex: stale plugin reference "codex" was found.

custom_base_entry_quiet_allow_warns:
  plugins.allow: plugin not installed: codex
  plugins.allow: stale plugin reference "codex" was found.
  no plugins.entries.codex warning lines

enabled_missing:
  plugins.entries.codex: plugin not installed: codex
  plugins.entries.codex: stale plugin reference "codex" was found.
```

Observed result after fix: diagnostics now follow the same resolved runtime policy as agent routing; intentional non-Codex routes are quiet, and explicit/stale Codex policy still reports actionable warnings.

What was not tested: full cross-OS CI and a real user credentialed home; the live proof used isolated local configs only.

## Tests

- `pnpm test -- --run src/agents/openai-routing.test.ts src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-plugin-config.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts`
  - `src/agents/openai-routing.test.ts`: 13 passed
  - `src/config/config.plugin-validation.test.ts`: 54 passed
  - `src/commands/doctor/shared/stale-plugin-config.test.ts`: 16 passed
  - `src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts` and `src/commands/agent.test.ts`: 132 passed together in the commands shard
  - `src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts`: 40 passed
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm deadcode:dependencies && pnpm deadcode:unused-files && pnpm deadcode:report:ci:ts-unused`
- `pnpm deps:shrinkwrap:check`
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings

target.title: fix(feishu): normalize unicode emojis to Feishu emoji type strings for reactions API (#66406)
target.body:
## Summary
Feishu reactions API returns error `231001` ("reaction type is invalid") because the agent sends raw unicode emojis (e.g. thumbs-up `👍`) but the API requires uppercase Feishu type strings like `THUMBSUP`.

## Root Cause
In `extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts`, the `emoji` parameter from the tool call is passed directly to `addReactionFeishu()` without conversion. The Feishu API only accepts its own emoji type vocabulary (e.g. `THUMBSUP`, `HEART`, `FIRE`) per https://open.feishu.cn/document/server-docs/im-v1/message-reaction/emojis-introduce, not unicode characters.

ProjectClownfish landed a narrow repair on this branch on 2026-04-28 (force-push by `vincentkoc`) so the original contributor path can stay canonical. The fix shape and tests have been stable since then.

## Changes
- `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts`: Add `normalizeFeishuEmoji()` function with a unicode-to-Feishu-type mapping table covering all common emoji reactions. Known Feishu type strings pass through unchanged; case-insensitive matching is supported.
- `extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts`: Apply `normalizeFeishuEmoji()` to the emoji parameter before calling `addReactionFeishu()` and `listReactionsFeishu()`.
- `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.test.ts`: Add test coverage for pass-through, case normalization, unicode conversion, whitespace trimming, and unknown emoji fallback.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Tool calls that pass unicode emoji to `addReactionFeishu` send `{ reaction: { emoji_type: "👍" } }` to the Feishu reactions HTTP API, which rejects it with `code: 231001` and `msg: "reaction type is invalid"`. After the fix the same tool call sends `{ reaction: { emoji_type: "THUMBSUP" } }`, which is the documented Feishu emoji type vocabulary. Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66406
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-66406` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. Because the worktree's `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts` imports run-time Feishu SDK modules transitively, the smoke script extracts the **production `FeishuEmoji` constant table and `normalizeFeishuEmoji` function body verbatim** from `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts` (SHA-256-pinned for integrity) and executes that exact slice via `node --experimental-strip-types`. No mocks, no test runner — the live function body is what runs. PR head: `a3bebf502f1ce0ebfe3b754ec1ab67b3302098fc`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/feishu-reaction-emoji-type && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr66478.mts`. The smoke script reads `extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts`, slices from `export const FeishuEmoji` to end-of-file, prints the prod-slice SHA-256, then exercises `normalizeFeishuEmoji` against the actual reporter unicode emojis.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3bebf502f` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, 2245-byte production slice verified by SHA-256 `8e5dd9eb5cc3406b058e81617e0085a2347b780ad8d49b3220aab683c243af54`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr66478.mts
$ wc -c C:\...\feishu-combined-1778492490068.mts
  bytes: 3667
(prod slice: 2245 bytes copied verbatim from extensions/feishu/src/reactions.ts starting at offset 3989)
(SHA-256 of prod slice for integrity:)
  8e5dd9eb5cc3406b058e81617e0085a2347b780ad8d49b3220aab683c243af54

=== Sample known Feishu emoji constants ===
FeishuEmoji.THUMBSUP = THUMBSUP
FeishuEmoji.HEART    = HEART
FeishuEmoji.CRY      = CRY

=== Unicode emoji -> Feishu type name ===
  "👍"             (thumbs up)  ->  THUMBSUP
  "❤️"             (red heart)  ->  HEART
  "😂"             (face with tears of joy)  ->  LAUGHING
  "🎉"             (party popper)  ->  PARTY
  "🙏"             (folded hands)  ->  PRAY
  "🔥"             (fire)  ->  FIRE

=== Bypass cases (must NOT be touched) ===
  "THUMBSUP"      -> "THUMBSUP"
  "thumbsup"      -> "THUMBSUP"
  "ThumbsUp"      -> "THUMBSUP"
  "  HEART  "     -> "HEART"
  "UNKNOWN_TYPE"  -> "UNKNOWN_TYPE"

=== Request body produced for issue #66406 reproducer ===
user sends: "👍"
request body: {"reaction":{"emoji_type":"THUMBSUP"}}
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: Each tested unicode emoji is mapped to a documented Feishu type name (`👍`→`THUMBSUP`, `❤️`→`HEART`, `😂`→`LAUGHING`, `🎉`→`PARTY`, `🙏`→`PRAY`, `🔥`→`FIRE`) — exactly the vocabulary listed at https://open.feishu.cn/document/server-docs/im-v1/message-reaction/emojis-introduce. Existing uppercase Feishu types (`THUMBSUP`), lowercase aliases (`thumbsup`), mixed case (`ThumbsUp`), and whitespace-padded inputs (`"  HEART  "`) all pass through to their canonical type name. Unknown inputs (`UNKNOWN_TYPE`) fall through unchanged, preserving forward-compat with new Feishu types. The final reproducer line confirms the runtime request body now contains `"emoji_type":"THUMBSUP"` — which is exactly what the Feishu API requires to avoid `code: 231001`.
- **What was not tested**: A live `addReactionFeishu` HTTP roundtrip to the actual Feishu Open Platform server with a paired bot token + chat_id is not exercised on the contributor machine (would need a configured Feishu workspace). The fix is a pure-function normalization at the request-builder boundary; downstream `addReactionFeishu` already serialises the `emoji_type` value verbatim, so the only behavioural change is that the request body now carries documented Feishu types instead of raw unicode. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live Feishu API recording is required; vincentkoc / ProjectClownfish already validated this fix shape via `pnpm -s vitest run extensions/feishu/src/reactions.test.ts` and the gated `pnpm check:changed` lanes when force-pushing on 2026-04-28.

## Test

- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/reactions.test.ts` — 5 cases (pass-through / case normalization / unicode conversion / whitespace trimming / unknown emoji fallback)
- `pnpm check:changed` — green (per vincentkoc's 2026-04-28 status update)

Closes #66406
